mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.0 percent, labor market deterioration triggers risk-off positioning and earnings downgrade expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal exhibits no predictive power in post-2008 data.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in consumer credit expansion (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust aggregate demand, which lifts corporate revenue growth expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in more than 50% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight labor markets, rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects capacity expansion rather than demand-pull inflation, which supports equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL annualized growth < 3.0%
regime_hint: tight labor market with accommodative inflation
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises alongside CPIAUCSL exceeding 3.0 percent annualized, indicating supply-side growth is being priced as inflationary rather than earnings-positive.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth (GDPC1) exceeding the historical trend rate of 2.5 percent signals sustained corporate earnings momentum, which mechanically supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of observations where GDPC1 exceeds 2.5 percent, or the rolling 1-year Spearman correlation with SPX returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, the cost of capital compresses equity risk premiums and forces portfolio rebalancing into equities, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as markets price in eventual policy normalization.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain negative or flat for more than 60 consecutive trading days after the rate threshold breach, or the forward return distribution shows no statistically significant shift from the pre-threshold baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and a declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to generate positive forward returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample windows, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for CPIAUCSL momentum.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary environment where CPIAUCSL growth decelerates while GDPC1 remains positive, real income gains accelerate, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL decelerates while GDPC1 annualized growth > 1.5%
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL deceleration coincides with GDPC1 falling below 1.0 percent annualized, indicating the signal captures demand destruction rather than real income growth.
